data.title: Philogen posts record 2025 profit on RayzeBio deal, plans dividend and R&D push
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Philogen SpA ( (IT:PHIL) ) is now available.

Philogen reported record 2025 results, with net profit surging to €229.7 million and revenue from contracts with customers jumping more than fourfold to €314.3 million, largely driven by a major upfront payment from a licensing deal with Bristol Myers Squibb’s RayzeBio unit for a prostate cancer candidate. The group’s EBITDA expanded to €261.6 million, its net financial position turned strongly positive at €368.3 million, and the board will propose allocating 2025 profit to a non-distributable reserve while funding a €0.70 per-share dividend from existing reserves, alongside seeking shareholder approval for its remuneration report and a new treasury share buyback authorization.

Management signalled that the strengthened balance sheet will support broader R&D and manufacturing investment, including seven new potentially registration-enabling studies by 2027 and multiple Phase I trials, underlining Philogen’s ambition to accelerate its pipeline toward commercialisation despite the absence of marketed products. The company is also expanding its artificial intelligence capabilities to boost operational efficiency and drug discovery, a move that could enhance its competitive position in biotech partnering as it continues to monetise its discovery platforms through high-value licensing agreements.

The most recent analyst rating on (IT:PHIL) stock is a Buy with a EUR24.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Philogen SpA stock, see the IT:PHIL Stock Forecast page.

More about Philogen SpA

Philogen S.p.A. is an Italian biotechnology group, together with its Swiss subsidiary Philochem AG, focused on discovering and developing antibody-based drugs and small organic molecules, particularly in oncology. Operating without products yet on the market, it generates revenue mainly from upfront payments, milestones and collaboration agreements with larger pharmaceutical partners, alongside contract research and manufacturing services.

Average Trading Volume: 17,818

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: €643.3M

See more insights into PHIL stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Odfjell SE Posts Solid 2025 Results Amid Softer Markets and Volatility
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Odfjell SE ( (GB:0J77) ) just unveiled an update.

Odfjell SE has published its 2025 annual report, highlighting solid financial results and operational performance in a year characterized by softer markets and geopolitical volatility. CEO Harald Fotland said the year underscored the strength of the company’s people and business model, emphasizing that responsible operations, team expertise, and long-term strategic choices continue to support Odfjell’s resilience and forward momentum.

The report package includes full financial statements for Odfjell Group and Odfjell SE, the Board of Directors’ report with a sustainability statement, and business reviews for Odfjell Tankers, Ship Management, and Odfjell Terminals. The publication offers stakeholders a comprehensive view of how the group navigated challenging market conditions while maintaining its position in the global chemical shipping and tank terminal markets.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:0J77) stock is a Hold with a NOK118.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Odfjell SE stock, see the GB:0J77 Stock Forecast page.

More about Odfjell SE

Odfjell SE is a leading global provider of seaborne transportation and storage of chemicals and other specialty bulk liquids, operating a fleet of around 70 ships serving both global and regional trades. The group also runs four strategically located tank terminals at major international shipping hubs, employs about 2,300 staff worldwide, and is headquartered in Bergen, Norway, with offices in 12 locations and 2025 gross revenue of USD 1,115 million.

Current Market Cap: NOK9.07B

data.title: Jushi Refinances Debt With $160 Million Term Loan, Bolstering Cash and Balance Sheet
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Jushi Holdings ( (TSE:JUSH) ) has shared an update.

Jushi Holdings Inc. has refinanced its former first and second lien credit facilities with a new US$160 million senior secured term loan led by funds managed by FocusGrowth Asset Management, providing non-dilutive financing that matures in three years and carries a 12.5% annual interest rate. Following the refinancing, Jushi reports approximately US$35 million in cash, with the transaction structured as a non-amortizing facility secured by first-priority liens on certain company and subsidiary assets.

The term loan includes significant participation from CEO and founder James Cacioppo and major shareholder Denis Arsenault, making it a related party transaction under Canadian securities rules, though exemptions from formal valuation and minority approval requirements were used and the deal was vetted by an independent special committee. Jushi also announced it will report its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results on March 31, 2026, offering investors an upcoming opportunity to assess the impact of the strengthened balance sheet and revised capital structure on the company’s performance.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:JUSH) stock is a Hold with a C$0.81 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Jushi Holdings stock, see the TSE:JUSH Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on JUSH Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, JUSH is a Neutral.

Jushi Holdings’ overall stock score reflects significant financial challenges, particularly in profitability and leverage, which are the most impactful factors. While there are positive signs in revenue growth and operational improvements, these are counterbalanced by increased net losses and valuation concerns. Technical indicators suggest a neutral market sentiment, and regulatory uncertainties add further risk.

To see Spark’s full report on JUSH stock, click here.

More about Jushi Holdings

Jushi Holdings Inc. is a vertically integrated, multi-state cannabis company focused on building a portfolio of branded cannabis assets across the U.S. through acquisitions, distressed workouts and competitive license applications. The company targets the full cannabis value chain, aiming to maximize shareholder value while delivering high-quality products across the cannabis ecosystem.

Average Trading Volume: 72,051

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: C$118M

Opawica Explorations ( (TSE:OPW) ) has issued an update.

Opawica Explorations Inc. has appointed its current chief financial officer, Monty Sutton, to the board of directors, effective March 25, 2026. Sutton brings more than 35 years of public markets, governance, administration and accounting experience, reinforcing the company’s leadership bench as it advances exploration efforts in Quebec’s Abitibi Gold Belt.

The company also noted that an independent Qualified Person has reviewed and approved the scientific and technical information supporting its exploration activities. This validation underscores Opawica’s adherence to regulatory standards as it pursues the conceptual mineral targets on its properties, which remain at an early, non-resource-defined stage.

data.title: International Care Company Turns EBITDA Positive on Revenue Growth and Strategic Shift
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

International Care Company S.p.A ( (IT:ICC) ) just unveiled an announcement.

International Care Company reported 2025 revenues of €9.25 million, up 9% year on year, and value of production of €9.73 million, while turning EBITDA positive at €331,000 versus a loss in 2024. Despite remaining in the red at the bottom line with a net loss of €566,000, the company swung from net financial debt to a small net cash position of €73,000 and will convene its ordinary shareholders’ meeting on 28 April 2026.

Growth was driven by strategic business lines in worldwide assistance and digital health-caregiving, partially offset by lost satellite and third-party claims management contracts and the postponement of some new agreements to 2026. Management highlighted improved operating efficiency, disciplined cost growth, continued investment in technology and governance, and sees the integration of digital health, personal care and international mobility risk management as a key driver to strengthen its competitive positioning in a challenging macroeconomic environment.

The most recent analyst rating on (IT:ICC) stock is a Buy with a EUR1.80 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on International Care Company S.p.A stock, see the IT:ICC Stock Forecast page.

More about International Care Company S.p.A

International Care Company S.p.A. is an Italian provider of care and risk management services listed on Euronext Growth Milan. The group focuses on digital health, tele-assistance, tele-consultation, caregiving and personal assistance, alongside integrated travel risk management and security solutions for insurance and corporate clients, targeting growing demand driven by European demographic and mobility trends.

Average Trading Volume: 5,718

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: €5.86M

data.title: Fino Payments Bank Withdraws Postal Ballot Item on CEO Reappointment Amid Ongoing Probe
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

FINO Payments Bank Ltd. ( (IN:FINOPB) ) has issued an update.

Fino Payments Bank has issued an addendum to its ongoing postal ballot, notifying shareholders that it is withdrawing one agenda item related to the re-appointment of Managing Director and CEO Rishi Gupta for a further three-year term. The move follows disclosures about Gupta’s arrest in an investigation involving certain program managers linked to multiple banks and a subsequent grant of bail by a special court, with the board citing due process and corporate governance as reasons for withdrawing only this specific proposal while leaving his candidature intact for reconsideration after regulatory reviews.

The postal ballot e-voting, which began on February 28 and runs through March 29, 2026, continues for all other agenda items, and the addendum has been circulated electronically and posted on the bank’s and exchanges’ websites. The bank emphasized that the investigation does not concern its GST compliance, and any future decision on Gupta’s re-appointment will depend on reassessment of his fit-and-proper status by the nomination committee and board, as well as the Reserve Bank of India’s view, signalling a cautious governance stance amid ongoing scrutiny.

More about FINO Payments Bank Ltd.

Fino Payments Bank Ltd. operates in India’s payments banking sector, offering deposit, remittance and digital banking services with a focus on technology-led financial inclusion. The bank serves retail and small customers through a network-driven model and is listed on both BSE and NSE, reflecting its role in the country’s emerging digital payments ecosystem.

Average Trading Volume: 32,774

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: 10.22B INR

data.title: Fuller’s Adds to Treasury Stock with Latest Share Buyback
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Fuller Smith & Turner ( (GB:FSTA) ).

Fuller, Smith & Turner P.L.C. has an issued share capital of 36,446,686 “A” Ordinary Shares of 40p each listed in London, of which 4,908,377 are held in Treasury and 31,538,309 constitute the current voting share base. These figures define the denominator shareholders must use when assessing whether changes in their holdings trigger disclosure obligations under U.K. transparency rules.

The company repurchased 15,000 “A” Ordinary Shares on 27 March 2026 via Deutsche Numis at an average price of about 649.73p as part of its ongoing share buyback programme. The newly acquired shares will be held in Treasury, marginally reducing the free float and voting rights in the market and continuing a capital management strategy that can support earnings per share and adjust the company’s equity structure over time.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:FSTA) stock is a Buy with a £847.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Fuller Smith & Turner stock, see the GB:FSTA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FSTA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FSTA is a Outperform.

Fuller Smith & Turner demonstrates strong technical momentum and positive corporate actions, which are the most significant factors driving the score. Financial performance is solid, though improvements in free cash flow and return on equity could enhance the outlook. Valuation is fair, providing a balanced risk-reward profile.

To see Spark’s full report on FSTA stock, click here.

More about Fuller Smith & Turner

Fuller, Smith & Turner P.L.C., commonly known as Fuller’s, is a U.K.-listed company with a capital structure comprising “A” Ordinary Shares of 40p each traded on the London Stock Exchange. The company maintains a portion of these shares in Treasury, which affects the total number of voting rights available to shareholders under FCA disclosure rules.

The company operates within the regulated U.K. capital markets framework and is subject to the FCA’s Disclosure and Transparency Rules, as well as the Market Abuse Regulation as incorporated into U.K. law. Its share structure and buyback activity are therefore closely monitored for implications on shareholder reporting obligations and market transparency.

Average Trading Volume: 34,562

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: £346.4M

data.title: Standard Life executives exercise incentive plan options and sell shares
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

Phoenix Group Holdings ( (GB:SDLF) ) has shared an update.

Standard Life has disclosed that Group Chief Executive Officer Andrew Briggs and Group General Counsel Quentin Zentner have exercised nil-cost options over ordinary shares under the company’s Long Term Incentive Plan and Deferred Bonus Share Scheme. The options, which vested after performance and holding periods tied to financial years up to 2023, resulted in Briggs retaining a portion of shares while selling others to meet tax and national insurance liabilities, whereas Zentner sold all shares acquired.

The transactions, conducted on 26 March 2026 on the London Stock Exchange at around 670 pence per share, form part of Standard Life’s routine executive remuneration and incentive structure. The disclosure, made under UK Market Abuse Regulation requirements, provides transparency on senior management equity dealings but does not signal any change in corporate strategy or governance beyond normal incentive plan operations.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:SDLF) stock is a Sell with a £6.60 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Phoenix Group Holdings stock, see the GB:SDLF Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SDLF is a Neutral.

Phoenix Group Holdings’ overall stock score is driven by strong earnings call results and positive corporate events, which highlight strategic progress and financial resilience. However, the mixed financial performance and valuation concerns due to ongoing profitability challenges temper the overall score. The technical analysis suggests a bullish trend, further supporting the stock’s potential.

Standard Life plc is a UK-based savings, investments and retirement company, providing long-term savings products, pensions and related financial services. data.title: British Smaller Companies VCT2 Buys Back Shares, Updates Voting Rights
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

British Smaller Companies VCT2 ( (GB:BSC) ) has issued an announcement.

British Smaller Companies VCT2 plc has repurchased 2,484,587 ordinary shares, representing 0.72% of its prior voting rights, at 52.18 pence per share, and will hold these shares in treasury. Following the transaction, the company’s issued share capital with voting rights stands at 342,471,654 ordinary shares, while it holds 38,696,133 shares in treasury, with the updated voting-share figure serving as the new reference point for investors’ disclosure obligations under FCA transparency rules.

The last reported net asset value was 54.40 pence per share as of 31 December 2025, indicating the buyback was executed at a modest discount to NAV. This buyback may be seen as part of the trust’s ongoing capital management strategy, potentially supporting the share price and providing clarity for shareholders on current voting rights and regulatory reporting thresholds.

More about British Smaller Companies VCT2

British Smaller Companies VCT2 plc is a UK-listed venture capital trust that invests in a portfolio of smaller British companies, providing growth capital and tax-efficient exposure for investors. The trust’s shares trade on the London market, and it actively manages its capital structure through share issuance and buyback activity.

Average Trading Volume: 4,697

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy
